title: MongoURL outside container
body:
Hello,

I have two kube files, one for mongo and one for another application. 

In the second kube deployment file, I have to specify the mongo url. In kubernetes it is something like <podname>.<namespace>.svc.cluster.local . But here how can I specify it? It doesn't seem to work with the container name or podman:container_name. 
```
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  creationTimestamp: "2021-02-03T12:49:07Z"
  labels:
    app: xxx
  name: mongodb_pod
spec:
  containers:
  - image: mongo:3.6
    name: mongodb
    env:
    - name: MONGO_INTERNAL_PORT
      value: "27017"
    - name: MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_USERNAME
      value: "root"
    - name: MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_PASSWORD
      value: "xxx"
    ports:
      - hostPort: 27017
        containerPort: 27017
        protocol: tcp
    command: ['-f', '/opt/mongod.conf']
    resources: {}
    securityContext:
      allowPrivilegeEscalation: true
      capabilities: {}
      privileged: false
      readOnlyRootFilesystem: false
    volumeMounts:
    - mountPath: /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d
      name: scripts
    - mountPath: /opt:
      name: config
    - mountPath: /data/db
      name: data
    workingDir: /
  volumes:
  - hostPath:
      path: ./files/mongo/scripts
      type: Directory
    name: scripts
  - hostPath:
      path: ./files/mongo/config
      type: Directory
    name: config
  - hostPath:
      path: mongodb_data_container
      type: Directory
    name: data
status: {}```

comments.body: @baude Is this something that dnsname would handle?
are you wanting this to work soley in podman or also in kube?
Only in podman. The both kube files are used only in Podman.

Hello @baude,

Thank you for you suggestion but I need a solution which can be delivered. I mean not changing the podman configuration. I also tried with the same pod kube file but it seems that the both containers receive the same ports even if I have:

```
      ports:
        - name: xx
          hostPort: 8081
          containerPort: 8081
          protocol: tcp
        - name: yyy
          hostPort: 27017
          containerPort: 27017
          protocol: tcp
```
the above feedback does not help me diagnose the problem for you ... did you exec into one of the pods and see if it can resolve by name ?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I am closing this do to lack of response.
